displayNotes: Use cards for scanability on iPhone: one primary number, status pill, and 2–3 details.
Use baseline/range visualization for HRV and RHR because personal baseline matters more than population comparison.
Use progress bars for readiness, sleep score, Body Battery, stress, steps, intensity minutes, training load, and nutrition.
This 5 AM run labels partial Garmin sync windows explicitly and marks unpopulated readiness rather than guessing.

Source fields include training readiness when available, HRV baseline, sleep score/duration/stages/need, Body Battery wake/current/high/charge/drain, stress average/max/rest split, steps/distance/active calories, weekly intensity minutes, acute load/ACWR when available, VO₂ max, nutrition calories/protein/water, resting HR vs 7-day average, SpO₂, and respiration.
